title: Dehaasia oblanceolata
taxonomy: Dehaasia oblanceolata is a species of plant in the family Lauraceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the laurel family. The Lauraceae family is native to various parts of the world, including Asia, Australia, and the Americas. The family is characterized by its diverse range of species, many of which are used in traditional medicine and have fragrant leaves. Dehaasia oblanceolata, in particular, is a species that is often found in tropical regions. Its classification within the Lauraceae family highlights the plant's unique characteristics and its place within the broader botanical hierarchy.

Additionally, it is also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions.

The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential in treating various health conditions such as wound healing, fever reduction, and cardiovascular diseases. The bioactive constituents of Dehaasia oblanceolata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
